NKJV.1: Praise the LORD!  Praise, O servants of the LORD, Praise the name of the LORD!
NKJV.2: Blessed be the name of the LORD From this time forth and forevermore!
NKJV.3: From the rising of the sun to its going down The LORD’s name is to be praised.
NKJV.4: The LORD is high above all nations, His glory above the heavens.
NKJV.5: Who is like the LORD our God, Who dwells on high,
NKJV.6: Who humbles Himself to behold The things that are in the heavens and in the earth?
NKJV.7: He raises the poor out of the dust, And lifts the needy out of the ash heap,
NKJV.8: That He may seat him with princes— With the princes of His people.
NKJV.9: He grants the barren woman a home, Like a joyful mother of children. Praise the LORD!

ESV.1: Praise the LORD! Praise, O servants of the LORD, praise the name of the LORD!
ESV.2: Blessed be the name of the LORD from this time forth and forevermore!
ESV.3: From the rising of the sun to its setting, the name of the LORD is to be praised!
ESV.4: The LORD is high above all nations, and his glory above the heavens!
ESV.5: Who is like the LORD our God, who is seated on high,
ESV.6: who looks far down on the heavens and the earth?
ESV.7: He raises the poor from the dust and lifts the needy from the ash heap,
ESV.8: to make them sit with princes, with the princes of his people.
ESV.9: He gives the barren woman a home, making her the joyous mother of children. Praise the LORD!

NASB.1: Praise the LORD! Praise, O servants of the LORD, Praise the name of the LORD.
NASB.2: Blessed be the name of the LORD From this time forth and forever.
NASB.3: From the rising of the sun to its setting The name of the LORD is to be praised.
NASB.4: The LORD is high above all nations; His glory is above the heavens.
NASB.5: Who is like the LORD our God, Who is enthroned on high,
NASB.6: Who humbles Himself to behold [The things that are] in heaven and in the earth?
NASB.7: He raises the poor from the dust And lifts the needy from the ash heap,
NASB.8: To make [them] sit with princes, With the princes of His people.
NASB.9: He makes the barren woman abide in the house [As] a joyful mother of children. Praise the LORD!

NLT.1:
Praise the LORD!
Yes, give praise, O servants of the LORD.
Praise the name of the LORD!
NLT.2:
Blessed be the name of the LORD
now and forever.
NLT.3:
Everywhere — from east to west —
praise the name of the LORD.
NLT.4:
For the LORD is high above the nations;
his glory is higher than the heavens.
NLT.5:
Who can be compared with the LORD our God,
who is enthroned on high?
NLT.6:
He stoops to look down
on heaven and on earth.
NLT.7:
He lifts the poor from the dust
and the needy from the garbage dump.
NLT.8:
He sets them among princes,
even the princes of his own people!
NLT.9:
He gives the childless woman a family,
making her a happy mother.
Praise the LORD!

WEB.1: Praise Yah! Praise, you servants of Yahweh, praise Yahweh’s name.
WEB.2: Blessed be Yahweh’s name, from this time forward and forever more.
WEB.3: From the rising of the sun to the going down of the same, Yahweh’s name is to be praised.
WEB.4: Yahweh is high above all nations, his glory above the heavens.
WEB.5: Who is like Yahweh, our God, who has his seat on high,
WEB.6: Who stoops down to see in heaven and in the earth?
WEB.7: He raises up the poor out of the dust. Lifts up the needy from the ash heap;
WEB.8: that he may set him with princes, even with the princes of his people.
WEB.9: He settles the barren woman in her home, as a joyful mother of children. Praise Yah!

CSB17.1:
Hallelujah!
Give praise, servants of the LORD;
praise the name of the LORD. ⓐ
CSB17.2:
Let the name of the LORD be blessed
both now and forever. ⓑ
CSB17.3:
From the rising of the sun to its setting,
let the name of the LORD be praised. ⓒ
CSB17.4:
The LORD is exalted above all the nations, ⓓ
his glory above the heavens. ⓔ
CSB17.5:
Who is like the LORD our God —
the one enthroned on high, ⓕ
CSB17.6:
who stoops down to look
on the heavens and the earth? ⓖ
CSB17.7:
He raises the poor from the dust
and lifts the needy from the trash heap ⓗ
CSB17.8:
in order to seat them with nobles —
with the nobles of his people. ⓘ
CSB17.9:
He gives the childless woman a household,
making her the joyful mother of children. ⓙ
Hallelujah!

AMP.1:
Praise the LORD! ( Hallelujah!)
Praise, O servants of the LORD,
Praise the name of the LORD.
AMP.2:
Blessed be the name of the LORD
From this time forth and forever.
AMP.3:
From the rising of the sun to its setting
The name of the LORD is to be praised [with awe-inspired reverence].
AMP.4:
The LORD is high above all nations,
And His glory above the heavens.
AMP.5:
Who is like the LORD our God,
Who is enthroned on high,
AMP.6:
Who humbles Himself to regard
The heavens and the earth?
AMP.7:
He raises the poor out of the dust
And lifts the needy from the ash heap,
AMP.8:
That He may seat them with princes,
With the princes of His people.
AMP.9:
He makes the barren woman live in the house
As a joyful mother of children.
Praise the LORD! (Hallelujah!)

MSG.1:
Hallelujah!
You who serve God, praise God!
Just to speak his name is praise!
MSG.2:
Just to remember God is a blessing—
now and tomorrow and always.
MSG.3:
From east to west, from dawn to dusk,
keep lifting all your praises to God!
MSG.4:
God is higher than anything and anyone,
outshining everything you can see in the skies.
MSG.5:
Who can compare with God, our God,
so majestically enthroned,
MSG.7:
He picks up the poor from out of the dirt,
rescues the wretched who've been thrown out with the trash,
MSG.8:
Seats them among the honored guests,
a place of honor among the brightest and best.
MSG.9:
He gives childless couples a family,
gives them joy as the parents of children.
Hallelujah!

NIV.1:
Psalm 113
Praise the Lord.
Praise, O servants of the Lord,
praise the name of the Lord.
NIV.2:
Let the name of the Lord be praised,
both now and forevermore.
NIV.3:
From the rising of the sun to the place where it sets,
the name of the Lord is to be praised.
NIV.4:
The Lord is exalted over all the nations,
his glory above the heavens.
NIV.5:
Who is like the Lord our God,
the One who sits enthroned on high,
NIV.6:
who stoops down to look
on the heavens and the earth?
NIV.7:
He raises the poor from the dust
and lifts the needy from the ash heap;
NIV.8:
he seats them with princes,
with the princes of their people.
NIV.9:
He settles the barren woman in her home
as a happy mother of children.
Praise the Lord.

NRSVCE.1:
Praise the LORD!
Praise, O servants of the LORD;
praise the name of the LORD.
NRSVCE.2:
Blessed be the name of the LORD
from this time on and forevermore.
NRSVCE.3:
From the rising of the sun to its setting

NRSVCE.4:
The LORD is high above all nations,

NRSVCE.5:
Who is like the LORD our God,

who looks far down

NRSVCE.7:
He raises the poor from the dust,

NET.1: Praise the LORD! Praise, you servants of the LORD, praise the name of the LORD!
NET.2: May the LORD’s name be praised now and forevermore!
NET.3: From east to west the LORD’s name is deserving of praise.
NET.4: The LORD is exalted over all the nations; his splendor reaches beyond the sky.
NET.5: Who can compare to the LORD our God, who sits on a high throne?
NET.6: He bends down to look at the sky and the earth.
NET.7: He raises the poor from the dirt, and lifts up the needy from the garbage pile,
NET.8: that he might seat him with princes, with the princes of his people.
NET.9: He makes the barren woman of the family a happy mother of children. Praise the LORD!
